feat: pixel art graphics — replace placeholder visuals (#14)

* chore: add GRAPHICS_PROMPT.md for art agent guidance

Documents art style (pixel art, top-down, Stardew Valley-ish), color
palette, all lot visual states (empty, hover, L1/L2 trailer, locked,
invalid flash), UI elements, and export requirements.

Unlocked by Milestone 4 completion per CLAUDE.md.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: replace placeholder graphics with pixel art sprites

Generates and integrates pixel art assets per prompts/GRAPHICS_PROMPT.md:

Sprites (game/assets/sprites/):
- lot_empty.png     — cracked dirt tile with pebbles and dry grass
- trailer_l1.png    — cream single-wide with windows, antenna, skirting
- trailer_l2.png    — gold upgraded trailer with flower box and awning
- icon_lock.png     — padlock with gold shackle for locked lots
- bg_park.png       — park background with fence, water tower, palm tree

UI icons (game/assets/ui/):
- icon_coin.png     — gold coin with $ symbol
- icon_account.png  — person silhouette for account button
- icon_quest.png    — parchment scroll with star badge

Code changes:
- LotGrid.gd: loads textures in _ready(), uses draw_texture_rect() for
  lot tiles and trailers, draws lock icon centered on locked lots,
  sets TEXTURE_FILTER_NEAREST for crisp pixel art rendering
- Main.tscn: replaces green ColorRect background with park background
  Sprite2D; keeps dark ColorRect underneath for screen edges

tools/generate_sprites.py saved for future sprite regeneration.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: load sprites via Image.load_from_file to bypass import system

Godot requires the editor to run once to generate .import files before
load() can find PNG resources. Using Image.load_from_file() +
ImageTexture.create_from_image() reads PNGs directly without import
files. Same fix applied to bg_park.png in Main.gd.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: revert to load() now that .import files exist

Godot editor generated .import sidecar files for all PNG assets.
Switching back from Image.load_from_file() to load() ensures sprites
work correctly on mobile export (APK/IPA).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
improve: harden all prompts for Claude opus-4-6

- Add shared JSON_OUTPUT_RULE header to all 5 prompts: explicit
  instruction to use \n escape sequences (not literal newlines) inside
  JSON string values — directly prevents the control-character parse error
  at the source rather than only catching it in sanitizeJsonControlChars()
- Fix feasibilityScorecard placeholder values: was literal 3 for all
  fields (ambiguous), now 0 with explicit note that values must be 1–5
- Strengthen executiveSummary \n\n instruction in business plan: now
  explicitly says 'the two-character escape sequence' to prevent literal
  newlines in the JSON string
- Promote math consistency rules in financial model to a named section
  with numbered invariants Claude must verify before outputting
- Trim business plan context passed to financial model stage: only sends
  financialSummary, assumptions, fundingOptions, milestones — saves
  ~2K tokens without losing any financially relevant context

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: unified naming (Skill/Kit/Collection/Catalog) + composition updates

Replaces jargon (monorepo/hybrid) with clear, marketable terms:
- Skill: one capability, one repo (atomic unit)
- Kit: curated bundle for a specific workflow (<domain>-kit)
- Collection: all skills in one repo (<domain>-skills)
- Catalog: org-level directory (README only)

publishing-strategy.md: restructured around new terminology
skill-composition.md: decision section uses Kit vs Collection

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Simplify verification: use --test-only and plugin logs, no bash -x

Verification now uses sbatch --test-only (validates without queuing)
and checks slurmctld logs for "bypass token valid" vs "wrapping job".
Simulates sandbox with prctl(no_new_privs) directly — no opaque
sandbox invocation needed.

commit.message:
Add admin Slurm wrappers with dry-run tests

System-wide sbatch/srun wrappers replace /usr/bin binaries. They read
the eBPF-protected bypass token to distinguish normal users (pass
through) from sandboxed processes (sandbox the job). Token injected
via env var only (never in /proc/*/cmdline). Manual _SANDBOX_BYPASS
in --export= is stripped.

- admin/sandbox-wrapper.conf: shared config (paths, token location)
- admin/srun-token-wrapper.sh: srun wrapper (pass through or sandbox)
- sbatch-sandbox.sh, srun-sandbox.sh: source sandbox-wrapper.conf
  to find relocated binaries when admin wrappers are deployed
- test.sh section 10: 12 dry-run admin wrapper tests, auto-detected
  via sandbox-wrapper.conf (no job submission — works on busy clusters)
- 119 passed, 0 failed, 7 skipped total

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: agent_sandbox
repository.description: Sandbox AI agents on HPC systems with slurm.

commit.message:
Fix tiering overflow and fuzzy dedup tag-mismatch gap

Tiering: group streams by resolution only (was resolution+packType).
The pack/single split doubled the tier slot budget per resolution —
1 top + 2 balanced + 2 efficient was being applied independently to
each sub-group, producing up to 10 streams at a single resolution
instead of 5.

Dedup: drop release tag from the fuzzy bucket key so streams where
one addon includes a tag (-tgx) and another omits it still end up
in the same bucket and are size-compared. Guard: if both candidates
carry known-but-different tags they are never merged (genuinely
different releases). Margin stays 0.5 GB when at least one tag is
known; falls back to 2-5% of file size when both tags are absent.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix LinkedIn token check: URL-encode credentials in curl request

The client_secret contains special characters (+, =) that get mangled
in application/x-www-form-urlencoded when using curl -d. Switch to
--data-urlencode so curl properly encodes each parameter.

commit.message:
Auto-detect Android Studio JDK for Gradle builds

Fixes "Unsupported class file major version" errors caused by the
system Java being too new for the Android Gradle Plugin.

- findAndroidStudioJdk() searches standard AS install paths on Linux,
  macOS, and Windows; respects ANDROID_STUDIO_JDK env var override
- Detected JAVA_HOME is injected into every Gradle spawn env
- JDK path shown in terminal output and Build Console panel (green badge)
- Warning shown if JDK not found (yellow badge + in-console hint)
- GET /api/jdk endpoint exposes detected JDK to the frontend
- README updated with JDK section, CLI commands, default file docs

commit.message:
Add tmap composition vignette with style registry integration (#26)

* Add Neexdzii Kwa spatial data and document all datasets

Generate subbasin data (Johnny David to Richfield on Bulkley mainstem,
~212 km²) via fresh package. Document both Bittner Creek and Neexdzii Kwa
datasets with roxygen.

Phase 1 of gq#17.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add tmap composition vignette for Neexdzii Kwa subbasin

Full map composition using registry styles: basemap blend, watershed,
streams, lakes, wetlands, roads, railway, keymap inset. All colors
from gq_reg_main(). Drop Z/M from bundled data for GEOS compat.

Stream/road widths use manual multiplier — proper scaling deferred
to gq#16.

Phase 2 of gq#17.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add gq_style() backend-agnostic resolver, name-based lookup

gq_style(reg, "lake") returns plain style lists (colors, widths,
classification) without tmap/mapgl dependency. gq_tmap_style() becomes
a thin wrapper adding tm_scale_categorical() for classified layers.

Both gq_tmap_style() and gq_tmap_classes() now accept name-based lookup
with automatic normalization ("Crossings - PSCIS assessment" ->
crossings_pscis_assessment). Backwards compatible with object-based calls.

Also adds Neexdzii Kwa habitat, crossings, fish obs, falls datasets and
updates composition vignette with all layers.

Relates to #21

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add field override, grouped category extraction, registry cleanup

- gq_style/gq_tmap_style/gq_tmap_classes gain `field` parameter to
  override classification field name for alternative data sources
  (e.g., bcfishpass barrier_status vs WHSE barrier_result_code)
- gq_qgs_extract handles QGIS grouped categories (#25) — expands
  multi-value symbols (Highway = RH1+RH2+RRP) into individual classes
- Re-extract reg_qgis_restoration.json and rebuild reg_main.json
  (roads_dra goes from 3 to 26 classified type codes)
- Rename gq_reg_read_csv() to gq_reg_custom()
- Rename reg_csv_custom.csv to reg_custom.csv
- Add inst/registry/xref_layers.csv for field name cross-reference
- Update vignette: drop column renames, use field parameter,
  roads now properly classified (highway yellow, arterial, local)
- 169 tests pass

Fixes #25

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Update task plan with completed phases

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(etherscan): add tokenbalance, getminedblocks, getLogs, supply, price actions (#12)

Add five missing Etherscan-compatible API actions needed by third-party tools:
- module=account&action=tokenbalance: ERC-20 token balance for an address
- module=account&action=getminedblocks: blocks produced by a validator
- module=logs&action=getLogs: event logs with block range and topic filters
- module=stats&action=ethsupply: total QFC supply
- module=stats&action=ethprice: QFC price structure (zeroed, no oracle)

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: validate address format in /address/:address endpoint (#20)

* feat(etherscan): add tokenbalance, getminedblocks, getLogs, supply, price actions

Add five missing Etherscan-compatible API actions needed by third-party tools:
- module=account&action=tokenbalance: ERC-20 token balance for an address
- module=account&action=getminedblocks: blocks produced by a validator
- module=logs&action=getLogs: event logs with block range and topic filters
- module=stats&action=ethsupply: total QFC supply
- module=stats&action=ethprice: QFC price structure (zeroed, no oracle)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add /agents routes for AgentRegistry queries

Adds three endpoints that query the AgentRegistry contract at
0x7791dfa4d489f3d524708cbc0caa8689b76322b3 via RPC:
- GET /agents — list recent agent registrations (eth_getLogs)
- GET /agents/:agentId — get agent details (eth_call getAgent)
- GET /agents/owner/:address — list agents by owner (eth_call getAgentsByOwner)

commit.message:
Add file search, default file, flavour build button, and ignore CLI

1. CLI: droidlane ignore <folder> [path]
   Appends a folder to .droidlane-ignore; no-ops if already present.

2. Default file (auto-open on startup)
   GET /api/default-file checks .droidlane-config.json first, then
   searches for release.gradle. POST /api/default-file saves the
   preference. Each file in the tree shows a ⊙ pin button on hover
   to set it as default. Dashboard auto-opens the default file on boot.

3. File search
   Search input above the explorer filters all files across the full
   tree as you type — shows filename + parent path as hint.

4. Flavour "Add to Build List" button
   When a file inside flavours/ is opened, a green button appears in
   the editor toolbar. Clicking it calls POST /api/flavour/apply which
   rewrites app/release.gradle to use that flavour (apply from + signing
   config lines), then opens release.gradle in the editor to confirm.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Add machine repair / finite population model example
- Add worked DTMC mean return time example (3-state chain, two methods)
- Add worked CTMC mean return time example (M/M/1 + HW4 embedded-vs-CTMC)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor: remove redundant ModelConfig struct

ModelConfig was duplicated in ModelRouteConfig as optional fields:
- temperature: f32 -> Option<f32>
- max_tokens: u32 -> Option<u32>
- stream: bool -> Option<bool>

The Settings.model field was never used anywhere in the codebase.
ModelRouteConfig already supports per-route model parameters.

Changes:
- Remove ModelConfig struct
- Remove Settings.model field
- Remove LegacySettings.model field
- Remove default_temperature(), default_max_tokens(), default_stream() helpers
- Remove ModelConfig::Default implementation

Net: -45 lines of redundant code.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Bundle static files to avoid GitHub rate limiting

The logo, desktop file, and metainfo XML were being downloaded from
GitHub on every install, which caused 429 Too Many Requests errors
in CI. Bundle them in the role's files/ directory and use
ansible.builtin.copy instead of get_url.
